intro: Apphud and Fyber sit on opposite sides of the transparency line — Apphud sells a subscription from $0 / month with a free tier, while Fyber only offers custom / contact-sales pricing. Apphud is in-app subscription analytics, A/B testing, and paywall tooling for mobile apps. Fyber is mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform (Digital Turbine). Pick Apphud when the job is mobile, apps and you accept medium lock-in; pick Fyber when it is existing fyber customers and publishers already using digital turbine's dt exchange and you accept high lock-in. The data we have shows Apphud at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Fyber at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Apphud is a poor fit for subscription-only apps, and Fyber is a poor fit for new publishers evaluating mediation in 2026, max or levelplay are better bets. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Apphud is for in-app subscription analytics; Fyber is for mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Apphud if a subscription at $0 / month and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to in-app subscription analytics, A/B testing, and paywall tooling for mobile apps.
verdict_b: Choose Fyber if deep integration is an acceptable price for the depth it buys and the work lines up with mobile ad mediation and in-app bidding platform (Digital Turbine).

faq.a: We can't verify it cleanly: Apphud starts at $0 / month (subscription), while Fyber (custom) doesn't publish an entry price. Request a quote from Fyber before assuming either side is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Apphud to Fyber?
faq.a: Moving from Apphud to Fyber is feasible, but Fyber is rated high lock-in — expect your integrations and workflows to need rework to match its model. Budget for a parallel-run period rather than a hard cutover.

Apphud scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 3/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Fyber a good alternative to Apphud?
faq.a: Yes — Fyber is a reasonable alternative to Apphud for mobile, apps. The practical differences are subscription-vs-custom billing and medium-vs-high lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Fyber as a credible swap.
